page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-90 is a compute-optimized server belonging to the c3d family. It features 90 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with x86_64 architecture, comprising 45 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 177.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU extended instructions scoring in the top 10%. The processor supports AVX-512 vector instructions, including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This hardware profile delivers top-tier software compilation and ray tracing performance, though memory write bandwidth for large blocks remains in the average tier.

This configuration is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis database operations, where it achieves top-tier throughput. It also handles small-to-medium language model inference efficiently, though performance drops to average for large 70B parameter models. The lack of local storage and a relatively low memory-to-vCPU ratio represent key tradeoffs for memory-intensive or storage-heavy applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-90 is a compute-optimized server featuring 90 dedicated vCPUs and 177.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with x86_64 architecture, it contains 45 physical cores and supports AVX-512 instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and Redis database throughput, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory read bandwidth is strong, though write bandwidth for large blocks remains average. With a resource density of 1.97 GB of RAM per vCPU, this server is designed for compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, and small-to-medium language model inference, while presenting a tradeoff for workloads requiring high memory capacity or local storage.

177.0 GB of system memory providing 1.97 GB per vCPU.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and cryptography performance.
AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration.
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis database operations.
